general_solar.fact: Decatur TX (33.23°N) average peak sun hours = 5.0–5.4/day annual avg; 5.8–6.4 in summer; 3.8 in December. Combined system derate factor ~0.77 for North TX conditions (heat, soiling, wiring losses).
general_solar.source: NREL PVWatts data for Decatur TX / engineering calculation

general_solar.fact: CoServ Electric (serves most of Wise County) offers net metering / distributed generation interconnection for residential systems under 25 kW. Approval timeline ~4–8 weeks. Retail rate export credit (verify current tariff). Requires bi-directional meter installed by CoServ at no charge.
general_solar.source: CoServ DG interconnection program (general knowledge — verify current tariff with CoServ directly)

general_solar.fact: Decatur TX residential solar requires building permit + electrical permit from City of Decatur Building Dept (within city limits) or Wise County. NEC 2020 adopted — 120% rule applies (NEC 705.12), 3-ft fire access path required on all roof planes.
N-Tech Energy Solutions has 4 pricing tiers — Starter: $2.40/W | Standard: $2.50/W | Premium: $2.70/W | Power+: $2.90/W
